name: El Gilillo Trail
name_original: Pico Gilillo por la Iruela - circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: Hike the difficult El Gilillo Trail, a 14.0-mile route with 3554 feet of elevation gain, offering panoramic views from Pico Gilillo.
region_intro: Hiking around La Iruela offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features mountainous terrain with significant peaks, river valleys like the Río Borosa and Guadalquivir, and deep canyons such as the Cerrada de Elías. Trails traverse varied ecosystems, including pine, yew, and ash forests, and often incorporate historical landmarks like the Castle of La Iruela. This area provides a range of hiking experiences from accessible paths to challenging, multi-hour treks. Best hiking trails around La Iruela The most popular hiking route is El Gilillo Trail, a 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail that takes 7 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends Pico Gilillo, offering expansive panoramic views of the surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Borosa Fold – Elías Gorge loop from Pliegue del Rio Borosa, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail follows the Río Borosa through the spectacular Cerrada de Elías gorge, featuring walkways and river views. Local hikers also love the Castle of La Iruela – Castillo de La Iruela loop from Cazorla, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through the historical area around the ancient castle, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Hiking around La Iruela is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, diverse forests, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in La Iruela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3600 hikers have used komoot to explore La Iruela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Iruela?
A: La Iruela offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in La Iruela?
A: The terrain around La Iruela is predominantly mountainous, situated at the foot of a rock formation. Trails often feature significant elevation changes, leading to panoramic views. You'll also encounter river valleys, deep canyons, and diverse forests of pines, yews, and ashes, reflecting the rich ecosystems of the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Iruela?
A: The Sierra de Cazorla region is beautiful year-round, but spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winters can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in La Iruela?
A: Yes, La Iruela has several easy routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Iruela – Castillo de La Iruela loop from Cazorla&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively short and accessible trail that explores the historical area around the ancient castle, offering both cultural interest and gentle walking.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in La Iruela?
A: Many trails in the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park are dog-friendly, but it's essential to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out, especially in protected areas, and ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Are there hiking routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its beautiful waterways and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the river – Linarejos Waterfall loop from Sierra de Cazorla&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the notable Cascada de Linarejos. You can also explore the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elías Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2613975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de los Órganos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around La Iruela?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borosa Fold – Elías Gorge loop from Pliegue del Rio Borosa&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Río Borosa through a stunning gorge. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Iruela – Castillo de La Iruela loop from Cazorla&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical sights.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking near La Iruela?
A: Hiking around La Iruela often integrates cultural and historical elements. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Iruela&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a steep hill, is a prominent landmark offering incredible views. You can also find the ruins of a Templar monastery and the Church of Santo Domingo, adding historical depth to your outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of La Iruela provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Many trails lead to panoramic viewpoints, such as "El Balcón del Paso del Aire," which offers an impressive overview of the diverse ecosystems and landscapes of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park.

Q: Can I spot wildlife on the trails in La Iruela?
A: The Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve renowned for its biodiversity. Hikers often have the chance to spot various wildlife, including deer and different species of birds of prey, especially in quieter areas and during dawn or dusk.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in La Iruela?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the diverse landscapes ranging from river valleys to mountain peaks, and the well-maintained trails that offer a mix of challenging and accessible options.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979760641/el-gilillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gilillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 14-mile route that ascends Pico Gilillo, rewarding hikers with expansive panoramic views of the surrounding natural park and requiring good physical condition.
tips: This 1848 meter peak is located in the Sierra de Cazorla natural park. At its foot is the beautiful town of Cazorla, from which you can ascend to this peak along one of its most popular routes.

This municipality in the province of Jaén, in Andalusia, is located on the slopes of the Sierra de Cazorla, crowning the Guadalquivir valley. One of its most emblematic monuments is its castle, dating from the 12th century and located at the top of a hill.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Gilillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The El Gilillo Trail is a full-day adventure. While the komoot data suggests an average duration of just under 8 hours, round trips can take anywhere from 6 to 9 hours or more, depending on your pace, the specific route variations taken, and how many stops you make to enjoy the views. It's best to start early.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Gilillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain. You'll start with forest sections, sometimes following scenic routes along rivers like the Rio Cerezuelo. As you ascend, it transitions to more open, exposed, rocky, and steep sections, especially closer to the summit. Expect to traverse large pine forests in lower elevations and a rough, rocky aesthetic higher up.

faqs.question: Is the El Gilillo Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, the El Gilillo Trail is generally classified as a medium-demanding to difficult hike, recommended for experienced hikers. It involves significant elevation gain, challenging sections, and can be poorly marked in places. It is not suitable for beginners or young children, and some parts with steep descents and vertical drops might not be suitable for those with a fear of heights.

faqs.question: What are the parking options and trailhead access for the El Gilillo Trail?
faqs.answer: While the specific trailhead isn't detailed, many routes to Pico Gilillo begin near La Iruela, often involving forest paths or routes along the Rio Cerezuelo. Parking availability can vary, so it's advisable to research specific starting points and parking areas in advance, especially during peak seasons. Arriving early is often a good strategy for parking.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the summit of Pico Gilillo?
faqs.answer: Reaching the summit of Pico Gilillo offers astounding 360-degree panoramic views. You can gaze upon the olive fields of Jaén province to the northwest, the Sierra Mágina to the west, and the Sierra de Segura to the northeast. The expansive vistas include mountains stretching as far as the eye can see, with several other 2000-meter peaks visible, making the effort truly worthwhile.

faqs.question: Is the El Gilillo Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. While dogs are generally permitted in natural parks in Spain, they must typically be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check the latest park regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the El Gilillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly beautiful during spring and autumn mornings. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter conditions can be challenging, with snow and ice making the final approach to the summit particularly difficult and potentially requiring specialized gear. Summer can be very hot, so an early start is crucial if hiking during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are permits or entrance fees required to hike the El Gilillo Trail or enter the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The El Gilillo Trail is located within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the natural park itself for hiking. However, certain activities or specific areas within the park might require permits, especially for large groups or specific research. For a standard hike, permits are typically not needed, but it's always wise to check the official park website for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What essential items should I bring for the El Gilillo Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its challenging nature and full-day duration, you should bring plenty of water, as natural sources are scarce along the route. Sturdy hiking shoes with good ankle support are essential, and trekking poles can be very helpful, especially on steep descents. A headlamp is recommended for longer hikes or if there's a chance of finishing after dark. A GPS device or app like komoot is highly recommended for navigation, as some sections can be poorly marked.

faqs.question: Does the El Gilillo Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'El Gilillo' trail itself (86%) and intersects with 'Variante 3 (1/2)' (59%), 'Puerto del Tejo - Arroyo de los Cierzos' (29%), and 'De Ermitas y Monasterios' (23%). You might also encounter shorter sections of trails like 'Cortados del Chorro' and 'Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén)'.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route besides Pico Gilillo?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning summit of Pico Gilillo, the trail's circular route often passes by or offers views of other interesting sites. One notable landmark in the vicinity, often visible or accessible from routes connected to El Gilillo, is the historic Castillo de La Iruela, a picturesque castle that adds to the cultural and scenic richness of the area.
